titles.title: AOC: A Snakemake workflow for the characterization of natural selection in protein-coding genes

institution.institution_name: Department of Systems and Computational Biomedicine, Weill Cornell Medicine, Cornell University, New York, NY 10021, United States of America
Institute for Genomics and Evolutionary Medicine, Temple University, Philadelphia, PA, United States of America

citation.article_title: HyPhy 2.5—a customizable platform for evolutionary hypothesis testing using phylogenies
citation.author: Kosakovsky Pond
citation.unstructured_citation: Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., Poon, A. F. Y., Velazquez, R., Weaver, S., Hepler, N. L., Murrell, B., & al., et. (2020). HyPhy 2.5—a customizable platform for evolutionary hypothesis testing using phylogenies. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 37(1), 295–299.

citation.author: Yang
citation.unstructured_citation: Yang, Z. (2007). PAML 4: Phylogenetic Analysis by Maximum Likelihood. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 24(8), 1586–1591. https://doi.org/10.1093/molbev/msm088

citation.author: Price

citation.article_title: Not so different after all: A comparison of methods for detecting amino acid sites under selection
citation.author: Kosakovsky Pond
citation.unstructured_citation: Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., & Frost, S. D. W. (2005). Not so different after all: A comparison of methods for detecting amino acid sites under selection. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 22(5), 1208–1222.

citation.article_title: Automated phylogenetic detection of recombination using a genetic algorithm
citation.author: Kosakovsky Pond
citation.unstructured_citation: Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., Posada, D., Gravenor, M. B., Woelk, C. H., & Frost, S. D. W. (2006). Automated phylogenetic detection of recombination using a genetic algorithm. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 23(10), 1891–1901.

citation.article_title: Contrast-FEL-a test for differences in selective pressures at individual sites among clades and sets of branches
citation.author: Kosakovsky Pond
citation.unstructured_citation: Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., Wisotsky, S. R., Escalante, A., Magalis, B. R., & Weaver, S. (2021). Contrast-FEL-a test for differences in selective pressures at individual sites among clades and sets of branches. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 38(3), 1184–1198.

citation.article_title: Evolutionary shortcuts via multi-nucleotide substitutions and their impact on natural selection analyses
citation.author: Lucaci
citation.unstructured_citation: Lucaci, A. G., Zehr, J. D., Enard, D., Thornton, J. W., & Kosakovsky Pond, S. L. (2023). Evolutionary shortcuts via multi-nucleotide substitutions and their impact on natural selection analyses. Molecular Biology and Evolution.

citation.article_title: The evolution of BDNF is defined by strict purifying selection and prodomain spatial coevolution, but what does it mean for human brain disease?
citation.author: Lucaci
citation.unstructured_citation: Lucaci, A. G., Notaras, M. J., Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., & Colak, D. (2022). The evolution of BDNF is defined by strict purifying selection and prodomain spatial coevolution, but what does it mean for human brain disease? Translational Psychiatry, 12(1), 1–17.

citation.article_title: Extra base hits: Widespread empirical support for instantaneous multiple-nucleotide changes
citation.author: Lucaci
citation.unstructured_citation: Lucaci, A. G., Wisotsky, S. R., Shank, S. D., Weaver, S., & Pond, S. L. K. (2021). Extra base hits: Widespread empirical support for instantaneous multiple-nucleotide changes. PLOS ONE, 16(3), e0248337.

citation.author: Martin

Selection analysis identifies clusters of unusual mutational changes in omicron lineage BA.1 that likely impact spike function. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 39(4), msac061.

citation.article_title: The emergence and ongoing convergent evolution of the SARS-CoV-2 N501Y lineages

citation.article_title: FUBAR: A fast, unconstrained bayesian AppRoximation for inferring selection
citation.author: Murrell
citation.unstructured_citation: Murrell, B., Moola, S., Mabona, A., Weighill, T., Sheward, D., Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., & al., et. (2013). FUBAR: A fast, unconstrained bayesian AppRoximation for inferring selection. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 30(5), 1196–1205.

citation.article_title: Detecting individual sites subject to episodic diversifying selection
citation.author: Murrell
citation.unstructured_citation: Murrell, B., Wertheim, J. O., Moola, S., Weighill, T., Scheffler, K., & Kosakovsky Pond, S. L. (2012). Detecting individual sites subject to episodic diversifying selection. PLoS Genetics, 8(7), e1002764.

citation.article_title: Spidermonkey: Rapid detection of co-evolving sites using bayesian graphical models

MACSE v2: Toolkit for the alignment of coding sequences accounting for frameshifts and stop codons. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 35(10), 2582–2584. https://doi.org/10.1093/molbev/msy159

The phylogenomics and evolutionary dynamics of the organellar genomes in carnivorous utricularia and genlisea species (lentibulariaceae). Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution, 181, 107711.

citation.article_title: Less is more: An adaptive branch-site random effects model for efficient detection of episodic diversifying selection
citation.author: Smith
citation.unstructured_citation: Smith, M. D., Wertheim, J. O., Weaver, S., Murrell, B., Scheffler, K., & Kosakovsky Pond, S. L. (2015). Less is more: An adaptive branch-site random effects model for efficient detection of episodic diversifying selection. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 32(5), 1342–1353.

citation.article_title: Evolution of viral genomes: Interplay between selection, recombination, and other forces
citation.author: Spielman
citation.journal_title: Evolutionary genomics: Statistical and computational methods
citation.unstructured_citation: Spielman, S. J., Weaver, S., Shank, S. D., Magalis, B. R., Li, M., & Kosakovsky Pond, S. L. (2019). Evolution of viral genomes: Interplay between selection, recombination, and other forces. In M. Anisimova (Ed.), Evolutionary genomics: Statistical and computational methods (pp. 427–468). Springer. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-1-4939-9074-0_14

citation.article_title: RELAX: Detecting relaxed selection in a phylogenetic framework
citation.author: Wertheim
citation.unstructured_citation: Wertheim, J. O., Murrell, B., Smith, M. D., Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., & Scheffler, K. (2015). RELAX: Detecting relaxed selection in a phylogenetic framework. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 32(3), 820–832.

citation.article_title: Synonymous site-to-site substitution rate variation dramatically inflates false positive rates of selection analyses: Ignore at your own peril
citation.author: Wisotsky
citation.unstructured_citation: Wisotsky, S. R., Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., Shank, S. D., & Muse, S. V. (2020). Synonymous site-to-site substitution rate variation dramatically inflates false positive rates of selection analyses: Ignore at your own peril. Molecular Biology and Evolution, 37(8), 2430–2439.

citation.article_title: Natural selection differences detected in key protein domains between non-pathogenic and pathogenic feline coronavirus phenotypes
citation.author: Zehr
citation.unstructured_citation: Zehr, J. D., Kosakovsky Pond, S. L., Millet, J. K., Olarte-Castillo, X. A., Lucaci, A. G., Shank, S. D., & al., et. (2023). Natural selection differences detected in key protein domains between non-pathogenic and pathogenic feline coronavirus phenotypes. Virus Evolution, 9(1), vead019.
